title: Arsenal FC vs. AFC Bournemouth
description: This event is for the upcoming Premier League game, scheduled for Saturday, April 11, 2026 between Arsenal FC and AFC Bournemouth.

markets.question: Will Arsenal FC win on 2026-04-11?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 11, 2026
If Arsenal FC wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. eventMetadata.context_description: Bournemouth's stunning 2-1 Premier League victory at Arsenal's Emirates Stadium on April 11 has locked in trader consensus at near-certainty for the Cherries' win, reflecting the official full-time result confirmed by match officials. Alex Scott's decisive second-half strike sealed back-to-back 1-2 triumphs for Bournemouth on this ground, 11 months apart, exposing Arsenal's nervy second-half collapse after Viktor Gyökeres equalized. The Gunners' title aspirations suffered amid flat play and inefficiency, as Mikel Arteta labeled a "big punch in the face." Minimal residual pricing on Arsenal or draw outcomes accounts for liquidity, with challenges limited to improbable VAR overhauls, protests, or replays absent any disruptions.

title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain holds a slim edge in trader consensus at 15.4% implied probability to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup, buoyed by their sustained FIFA world number one ranking, flawless European qualification campaign, and lingering momentum from Euro 2024 success with stars like Lamine Yamal shining. France trails closely at 14.6% after a 2-1 friendly victory over Brazil on March 26, highlighting Kylian Mbappé's form and defensive resilience, while England (11.3%), Argentina (8.9% as CONMEBOL leaders), and Brazil (8.6%) cluster tightly amid balanced squads and favorable group stage draws from December that delay top clashes until knockouts. The expanded 48-team field amplifies upset potential, but no major injuries or qualifier stumbles keep elite contenders' paths realistic in this hyper-competitive landscape.

title: The Masters 2026: Winner
description:
This polymarket will resolve according to the player who wins the 2026 Masters Tournament.

If this player is eliminated from contention for The Masters based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by The Masters official tournament rules.

If no winner is announced by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution sources will be the official results published by the PGA TOUR website and The Masters website. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rory McIlroy's record six-shot lead at 12-under par after a dominant second-round 65 at Augusta National has propelled his implied probability to 70.5% as defending champion chasing back-to-back green jackets, the first since Tiger Woods in 2001-02. Traders' consensus reflects his flawless ball-striking and putting on the firming course amid hot, dry weekend weather forecasts that could widen the gap on Moving Day. Chasers like Tommy Fleetwood, Justin Rose, Sam Burns, Patrick Reed, and Shane Lowry sit six back at six-under, buoying their mid-single-digit odds with solid 36-hole positioning and Augusta course history, while pre-tournament favorites Scottie Scheffler and others faltered early, dropping their probabilities below 3%. Upsets remain possible given Augusta's back-nine volatility.

eventMetadata.context_description: California Governor Gavin Newsom commands trader consensus as the frontrunner for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination, buoyed by recent polling leads in California (28%) and a tie for second in New Hampshire, alongside his national book tour and vocal opposition to President Trump that has elevated his profile among party insiders. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez garners progressive enthusiasm through viral media presence and ideological clarity, while Georgia Senator Jon Ossoff benefits from swing-state credentials and a February anti-Trump speech that went viral. Former Vice President Kamala Harris's recent remark to activists that she's "thinking about" another run has stirred speculation but failed to lift her standing amid her 2024 defeat. In this fragmented field, 2026 midterm results, fundraising hauls, and early endorsements from key factions could consolidate support, with no incumbent or clear favorite locking in dominance yet.

title: Military action against Iran ends on...?
description:
This market will resolve according to the next date Iran Standard Time (GMT+3:30), on which neither the US nor Israel initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Iranian soil or any official Iranian embassy or consulate. 

If the date/time of a qualifying strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar day after the listed date, the respective market will resolve to "Yes" regardless of whether a strike is later confirmed to have occurred. If strikes could be verified under the prior rule for each listed day, this market will resolve to "Military action through April 30."

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by US or Israeli military forces that impact Iranian ground territory or any official Iranian embassy or consulate (e.g., if a weapons depot on Iranian soil is hit by an US/Israeli missile, this will qualify).

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be considered, regardless of whether they land on Iranian territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US/Israeli ground operatives will not be considered.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by US or Israeli military forces that impact Iranian ground territory or any official Iranian embassy or consulate (e.g., if a weapons depot on Iranian soil is hit by an US/Israeli missile, this will qualify).

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be considered, regardless of whether they land on Iranian territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US/Israeli ground operatives will not be considered.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

series.title: Military action against Iran ends on

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors April 9 (98%) as the end date for US/Israel military action against Iran, driven by the April 7 US-Iran two-week ceasefire brokered by Pakistan, with no verified airstrikes on Iranian soil since prior to that agreement. This de-escalation signal was reinforced on April 9 when House Republicans blocked a war powers resolution limiting further operations, amid reports of Iran's ceasefire acceptance and upcoming Islamabad negotiations. The market's narrow definition—focusing on aerial strikes on Iranian territory—bolsters this positioning, reflecting the absence of post-ceasefire escalation. Realistic challenges include Iranian retaliation threats over Israeli strikes on Hezbollah in Lebanon, Strait of Hormuz tensions, or diplomatic breakdowns that could prompt renewed airstrikes before April 30 resolution.

title: Liverpool FC vs. Fulham FC
description: This event is for the upcoming Premier League game, scheduled for Saturday, April 11, 2026 between Liverpool FC and Fulham FC.

markets.question: Will Liverpool FC win on 2026-04-11?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 11, 2026
If Liverpool FC wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Liverpool's 57.5% implied probability as trader consensus favorite stems from their strong home record at Anfield and dominant head-to-head history against Fulham, whom they've beaten in most recent Premier League meetings, despite Liverpool sitting fifth in the table to Fulham's ninth. Key returns like Mohamed Salah, now fit after recent doubts, bolster the attack alongside Gakpo and Wirtz, while Virgil van Dijk and Ibrahima Konate anchor the defense; however, injuries sideline Alisson Becker long-term—with Giorgi Mamardashvili deputizing—plus Conor Bradley, Giovanni Leoni, and others, following midweek Champions League exertions in Paris. Fulham's solid mid-table form and absences like Kenny Tete contribute to the 17.5% underdog pricing and 25.5% draw likelihood in this tightly contested Premier League clash.

title: Next Prime Minister of Hungary
description:
Parliamentary elections are expected to be held in Hungary on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve to the individual who is next officially appointed and confirmed as Prime Minister of Hungary following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally elected and appointed to the role of Prime Minister. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is confirmed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Hungary; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Péter Magyar at 71.5% implied probability to become Hungary's next Prime Minister, driven by his Tisza party's commanding lead in recent opinion polls ahead of the April 12 parliamentary election. The latest Medián survey shows Tisza at 58% among decided voters versus Viktor Orbán's Fidesz at 35%, a 23-point gap widened from February, fueled by massive anti-Orbán rallies like yesterday's tens-of-thousands-strong gathering at Budapest's Heroes' Square and widespread voter fatigue after 16 years of Fidesz dominance. Orbán, seeking a fifth term, benefits from an electoral system favoring incumbents but trails in most independent polls; U.S. Vice President Vance's recent Budapest visit aimed to bolster him. A Tisza majority in the 199-seat National Assembly would position Magyar to form government, though coalition dynamics or recounts could influence the outcome.

title: US x Iran diplomatic meeting by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify. 

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran, and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Direct US-Iran peace talks began April 11, 2026, in Islamabad, Pakistan, with Vice President JD Vance leading the US delegation alongside Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi and parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, hosted by Pakistani mediators following a two-week ceasefire agreed around April 7 amid a six-week war. This high-level bilateral engagement, the most significant since 2025 nuclear negotiations, stems from recent US pressure on reopening the Strait of Hormuz and Tehran's precondition warnings, reflecting cautious optimism despite gaps on sanctions and nuclear limits. Traders monitor for de-escalation signals or breakdowns, with ongoing sessions potentially extending the truce or advancing diplomacy before the ceasefire expires mid-April.

title: Counter-Strike: Astralis vs FUT Esports (BO5) - PGL Bucharest Playoffs
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Grand final match between Astralis and FUT Esports in the PGL Bucharest Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 11 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Astralis" if Astralis win the match against FUT Esports.

This market will resolve to "FUT Esports" if FUT Esports win the match against Astralis.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Counter-Strike: Astralis vs FUT Esports - Map 4 Winner
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Grand final match between Astralis and FUT Esports in the PGL Bucharest Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 11 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Astralis" if Astralis win Map 4 against FUT Esports.

This market will resolve to "FUT Esports" if FUT Esports win Map 4 against Astralis.

If the match begins but is not completed, and Map 4 is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on the completed Map 4. 

If Map 4 is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. 

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Games Total: O/U 3.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Grand final match between Astralis and FUT Esports in the PGL Bucharest Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 11 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if Astralis and FUT Esports play 4 or more maps in this series.

If fewer than 4 maps are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Map 2: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 2, or if Map 2 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Map 5: Odd/Even Total Rounds?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined rounds played by both teams in Map 5 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined rounds played by both teams in Map 5 is an even number.

"Total combined rounds" is the sum of rounds won by both teams during the match, including any overtime rounds if applicable. For example, a match ending 13–11 would have a total of 24 rounds (even), while a match ending 13–10 would have a total of 23 rounds (odd).

If Map 5 is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

markets.question: Map 2: Odd/Even Total Rounds?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined rounds played by both teams in Map 2 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined rounds played by both teams in Map 2 is an even number.

"Total combined rounds" is the sum of rounds won by both teams during the match, including any overtime rounds if applicable. For example, a match ending 13–11 would have a total of 24 rounds (even), while a match ending 13–10 would have a total of 23 rounds (odd).

If Map 2 is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

markets.question: Map Handicap: AST (-2.5) vs FUT Esports (+2.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Grand final match between Astralis and FUT Esports in the PGL Bucharest Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 11 at 10:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Astralis" if Astralis wins 3 or more maps than FUT Esports in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "FUT Esports". 

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.
markets.groupItemTitle: Map Handicap: AST (-2.5) vs FUT Esports (+2.5)

eventMetadata.context_description: Both Astralis and FUT Esports enter the PGL Bucharest 2026 grand final unbeaten in playoffs after dominant semifinal wins—Astralis edged 3DMAX 2-0 on Dust2 (13-11) and Overpass (13-10) with phzy (1.47 rating) and HooXi (1.45 rating, Tec9 triple) shining in clutches, while FUT dispatched TheMongolZ 2-0 on Nuke and Mirage led by lauNX. Astralis rides a six-match win streak with strong Mirage (77% win rate) and Nuke (83%) pools, facing FUT's five-match streak and Overpass dominance (86%). Recent head-to-head favors Astralis (recent 2-1 ESL Pro League win), but BO5 vetoes and young FUT core (avg. 1.12 HLTV rating) heighten upset potential in this $1.25M showdown.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder lead at 40.5% implied probability after clinching the NBA's top overall seed and home-court advantage throughout the playoffs with a league-best 64-16 record, fueled by Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's MVP frontrunner status and Chet Holmgren's two-way dominance despite a recent 127-107 loss to surging Denver Nuggets. San Antonio Spurs sit at 15.8% on Victor Wembanyama's MVP-caliber explosion—including 40 points and 13 rebounds in Thursday's 139-120 rout of Dallas—securing West No. 2 seeding at 61-19 amid a franchise-record win pace. Boston Celtics hold 11.8% as Eastern powerhouses with 55 wins, while Nuggets' 11th straight victory boosts their 10.5% share; Detroit Pistons top East standings at 59-22 but lag at 5.8% due to postseason inexperience.

title: Bitcoin above ___ on April 11?
description:

title: Presidential Election Winner 2028
description:
The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

markets.question: Will Eric Trump win the 2028 US Presidential Election?
markets.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance holds a slim edge in trader consensus for the 2028 presidential election at 18.8%, ahead of California Governor Gavin Newsom at 16.1%, as his incumbency advantage and dominance in early Republican primary polls—like a February New Hampshire survey and March JL Partners data showing him leading by wide margins—bolster his frontrunner status amid President Trump's term limits. Newsom's competitive position stems from leading some Democratic nomination odds and appeal to young voters in recent tracking polls, while Marco Rubio's rise to 9.8% reflects his visibility as Secretary of State during recent diplomatic developments, including a ceasefire announcement that steadied Vance's odds. The tight race underscores open primaries, volatile early polling such as UMass Lowell's late-March head-to-head showing Vance edging Newsom 33%-30%, and potential shifts from 2026 midterms, endorsements, or scandals.

title: Fed decision in April?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders, wagering real capital, price a 98.3% implied probability of no Federal Reserve rate change at the April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting, reflecting the March CPI surge to 3.3% year-over-year—up sharply from February's 2.4% amid energy price spikes from geopolitical tensions. The Fed's March decision held the federal funds rate at 3.50%-3.75%, with dot plot signaling just one cut later in 2026, while minutes revealed growing openness to hikes if inflation persists. Solid nonfarm payrolls further solidified the pause consensus. A challenge would require softer April CPI/PCE data or labor market weakening to revive cut odds before resolution.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Republican Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Republican Party sources.

Any replacement of the Republican nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. as the narrow 2028 Republican presidential nominee frontrunner at 49%, just ahead of Vice President J.D. Vance at 40%, with Secretary of State Marco Rubio third at 22% in a fluid early primary field shaped by the Trump administration's high-profile cabinet. RFK Jr.'s edge reflects his surging MAHA health movement appeal to the GOP base, family statements confirming his "definite" 2028 bid, and Polymarket momentum from early March amid speculation fueled by PAC moves. Recent CPAC and New Hampshire straw polls favor Vance's incumbency advantage, highlighting market divergence from activist sentiment. Midterm outcomes, Trump endorsements, and policy successes could widen gaps before Iowa caucuses.

title: LoL: G2 Esports vs Team Vitality (BO3) - LEC Regular Season
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between G2 Esports and Team Vitality in the LEC Regular Season, initially scheduled for April 11 at 10:15AM ET.

This market will resolve to "G2 Esports" if G2 Esports win the match against Team Vitality.

This market will resolve to "Team Vitality" if Team Vitality win the match against G2 Esports.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Games Total: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between G2 Esports and Team Vitality in the LEC Regular Season, initially scheduled for April 11 at 10:15AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if G2 Esports and Team Vitality play 3 or more games in this series.

If fewer than 3 games are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both G2 Esports and Team Vitality each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both G2 Esports and Team Vitality each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Any Player Quadra Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. In League of Legends, a Quadra Kill occurs when a single player kills 4 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Quadra Kill. A Penta Kill (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as a Quadra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether a Quadra Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Quadra Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all champion kills recorded during the match by both G2 Esports and Team Vitality. Executions (deaths to non-champion sources such as turrets, minions, or neutral monsters where no enemy champion receives kill credit) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Game 1, or if Game 1 is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 3: Any Player Quadra Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 3. In League of Legends, a Quadra Kill occurs when a single player kills 4 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 3. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Quadra Kill. A Penta Kill (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as a Quadra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 3 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether a Quadra Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Quadra Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 3 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 3: Any Player Penta Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Penta Kill during Game 3. In League of Legends, a Penta Kill occurs when a single player kills all 5 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Penta Kill during Game 3. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Penta Kill.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 3 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether a Penta Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Penta Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 3 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 3: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 3 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 3 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all champion kills recorded during the match by both G2 Esports and Team Vitality. Executions (deaths to non-champion sources such as turrets, minions, or neutral monsters where no enemy champion receives kill credit) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Game 3, or if Game 3 is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 3 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Game Handicap: VIT (-1.5) vs G2 Esports (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between G2 Esports and Team Vitality in the LEC Regular Season, initially scheduled for April 11 at 9:55AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Team Vitality" if Team Vitality wins 2 or more games than G2 Esports in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "G2 Esports". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: G2 Esports tops LEC Spring Split 2026 power rankings with consistent early macro execution and a dominant 24-11 head-to-head edge over Team Vitality, including a recent Versus split win, driving trader preference in this regular season BO3. Vitality ranks fifth but shows upset potential after a 2-0 sweep last week and strong 3-1 group stage record, bolstered by new midlaner Humanoid's integration alongside Carzzy's carry threat. Both rosters remain intact without injury concerns, emphasizing draft adaptability in the current patch favoring aggressive sidelanes. Tight standings heighten stakes for playoff seeding in the double-elimination format.

eventMetadata.context_description: Barcelona's commanding position atop La Liga standings with 76 points from 30 games, a six-point lead over Real Madrid, and an impressive 17-match unbeaten run in the league underpin trader consensus pricing them at 96.5% to defeat mid-table Espanyol (10th, 38 points) in this Catalan derby at Spotify Camp Nou. Hansi Flick's side seeks a rebound from their midweek 0-2 Champions League loss to Atletico Madrid, bolstered by Frenkie de Jong's return to group training after a hamstring issue, despite absences like Raphinha (hamstring) and Andreas Christensen. Espanyol's winless streak in their last five La Liga outings and historical head-to-head struggles—Barcelona unbeaten in recent derbies bar draws—cement the gap. Upsets remain possible via Barca rotations, fatigue, or another goalkeeper masterclass like their reverse fixture stalemate.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Brentford FC vs. Everton FC Premier League match at Gtech Community Stadium concluded in a 2-2 draw, driving trader consensus to price the Draw outcome at 100% upon official resolution. Igor Thiago's brace put the hosts ahead twice, but Kieran Dewsbury-Hall's stoppage-time equalizer salvaged a point for Everton, who entered level on points with Brentford but trailing on goal difference. This result caps a competitive mid-table battle, following Brentford's 4-2 win over Everton in January's reverse fixture. With the scoreline confirmed by the league, no realistic challenges remain barring rare disputes over video review or administrative errors.

title: Rolex Monte Carlo Masters: Carlos Alcaraz vs Valentin Vacherot
description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Carlos Alcaraz and Valentin Vacherot in the Rolex Monte Carlo Masters, scheduled for April 11 at 9:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Carlos Alcaraz' if Carlos Alcaraz advances against Valentin Vacherot.

This market will resolve to 'Valentin Vacherot' if Valentin Vacherot advances against Carlos Alcaraz.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.groupItemTitle: Rolex Monte Carlo Masters: Carlos Alcaraz vs Valentin Vacherot Match O/U 23.5

eventMetadata.context_description: World No. 1 Carlos Alcaraz holds a commanding edge in the Rolex Monte Carlo Masters semifinals against home wildcard Valentin Vacherot, reflecting trader consensus on Alcaraz's superior clay-court prowess and tournament form. Alcaraz, pursuing a title defense after straight-sets wins over Sebastian Baez, Tomas Martin Etcheverry, and Alexander Bublik, boasts a stellar Masters 1000 clay record including Madrid and Roland Garros triumphs. Vacherot, ranked No. 23 with a career-high this week, stunned the field by upsetting two Top 10 players including Alex de Minaur in the quarters for his first Monte Carlo semifinal as the first Monegasque to reach this stage. No head-to-head exists, but Vacherot's crowd-fueled momentum on Court Rainier III faces Alcaraz's baseline dominance and big-match experience, with no reported injuries affecting either.

title: LoL: Top Esports vs JD Gaming (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Top Esports and JD Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 11 at 7:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Top Esports" if Top Esports win the match against JD Gaming.

This market will resolve to "JD Gaming" if JD Gaming win the match against Top Esports.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Top Esports and JD Gaming each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Top Esports and JD Gaming each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Any Player Quadra Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. In League of Legends, a Quadra Kill occurs when a single player kills 4 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Quadra Kill. A Penta Kill (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as a Quadra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.description:

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 3: Any Player Quadra Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 3. In League of Legends, a Quadra Kill occurs when a single player kills 4 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 3. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Quadra Kill. A Penta Kill (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as a Quadra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 3 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether a Quadra Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Quadra Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 3 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 3: Any Player Penta Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Penta Kill during Game 3. In League of Legends, a Penta Kill occurs when a single player kills all 5 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Penta Kill during Game 3. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Penta Kill.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 3 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether a Penta Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Penta Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 3 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 3: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 3 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 3 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all champion kills recorded during the match by both Top Esports and JD Gaming. Executions (deaths to non-champion sources such as turrets, minions, or neutral monsters where no enemy champion receives kill credit) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Game 3, or if Game 3 is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 3 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Game Handicap: TES (-1.5) vs JD Gaming (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Top Esports and JD Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 11 at 7:20AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Top Esports" if Top Esports wins 2 or more games than JD Gaming in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "JD Gaming". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In the upcoming match between Top Esports and JD Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 11 at 7:20AM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 1.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 1 is 37 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Top Esports enters LPL Split 2 Group Ascend BO3 against JD Gaming with strong momentum from recent 2-0 sweeps over Anyone's Legend and Team WE, boosting their 2-1 record amid Creme's standout mid-lane performances on skirmishers like Aurora and Ahri, alongside JackeyLove's carry threat. JD Gaming, sitting 1-1 after a 2-0 win over AL but a 1-2 loss to BLG, leverages Split 1 playoff edge where they ousted TES 3-2, with Xiaoxu anchoring top lane stability. Head-to-head favors TES 16-13 overall in a competitive rivalry; double round-robin format heightens stakes for direct playoff bracket qualification in Patch 26.7's macro-focused meta emphasizing objective control and teamfights. No roster changes or withdrawals reported.

title: LoL: Team WE vs Invictus Gaming (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Team WE and Invictus Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 11 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Team WE" if Team WE win the match against Invictus Gaming.

This market will resolve to "Invictus Gaming" if Invictus Gaming win the match against Team WE.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Games Total: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Team WE and Invictus Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 11 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if Team WE and Invictus Gaming play 3 or more games in this series.

If fewer than 3 games are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Team WE and Invictus Gaming each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Team WE and Invictus Gaming each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Destroy Inhibitors?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 1. In League of Legends, each team has three inhibitors (one in each lane), located behind the inner turrets.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Team WE and Invictus Gaming each destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) do not destroy any enemy inhibitors.

eventMetadata.context_description: Invictus Gaming leads trader consensus in this LPL Split 2 Group Ascend BO3 matchup, buoyed by their perfect 2-0 record highlighted by a gritty 2-1 comeback over Ninjas in Pyjamas three days ago, where jungler Wei's aggressive paths dictated tempo. Veteran midlaner Rookie and import top laner Soboro have meshed seamlessly in the current patch favoring early skirmishes, contrasting Team WE's 0-3 skid capped by a 0-2 defeat to Top Esports on April 8 amid consistent laning phase struggles. WE's young core—jungler Monki, midlaner Karis, ADC About—shows promise but lacks cohesion against IG's experience in the double round-robin format, with no reported roster or sub issues for either side.

title: FC St. Pauli 1910 vs. FC Bayern München
description: This event is for the upcoming Bundesliga game, scheduled for Saturday, April 11, 2026 between FC St. Pauli 1910 and FC Bayern München.

markets.question: Will FC St. Pauli 1910 win on 2026-04-11?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 11, 2026
If FC St. Pauli 1910 wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Bayern München's commanding 87.5% implied probability stems from their Bundesliga-leading position with 73 points and +73 goal difference after 28 matches, contrasting FC St. Pauli's relegation skirmishes marked by recent losses to Freiburg and Borussia Mönchengladbach. Bayern's squad depth shines despite rotations ahead of Champions League duties—resting Kane, Upamecano, and others while fielding Neuer, Kimmich, Musiala, Olise, and Guerreiro—bolstered by an 8-1 head-to-head edge, including a 3-1 win in November 2025. St. Pauli's injuries to Sands, Saliakas, and Smith weaken their setup at Millerntor-Stadion. An upset could arise from Bayern's injury fragility (Gnabry out with knee issues) or home crowd-fueled counters, though trader consensus sees slim 2.4% odds for the hosts.

title: Rolex Monte Carlo Masters: Alexander Zverev vs Jannik Sinner
description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Alexander Zverev and Jannik Sinner in the Rolex Monte Carlo Masters, scheduled for April 11 at 7:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Alexander Zverev' if Alexander Zverev advances against Jannik Sinner.

This market will resolve to 'Jannik Sinner' if Jannik Sinner advances against Alexander Zverev.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: World No. 2 Jannik Sinner holds a commanding 8-4 head-to-head edge over No. 3 Alexander Zverev, including victories in their last four ATP Masters 1000 meetings and a 1-1 clay-court split, fueling trader consensus toward the Italian in this Monte Carlo Masters semifinal on red clay. Sinner advanced convincingly past Felix Auger-Aliassime in the quarterfinals yesterday, extending a strong resurgence after early-season hiccups, while Zverev grinded through scares against Cristian Garin and Joao Fonseca, showcasing resilience but exposing fatigue risks on the demanding surface. Both excel on clay—Zverev with deep Monte Carlo runs historically—but Sinner's superior recent form, baseline power, and serving edge on this outdoor court position him as the trader-favored contender ahead of a potential final against Carlos Alcaraz.

title: Peru Presidential Election Winner
description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Peru on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who wins the next Peruvian Presidential election. 

This market includes any potential second round. 

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the results of this election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve solely on the official results as reported by the Peruvian government, specifically the National Office of Electoral Processes (Oficina Nacional de Procesos Electorales, ONPE) (https://www.onpe.gob.pe/elecciones/) and the National Jury of Elections (Jurado Nacional de Elecciones, JNE) (https://portal.jne.gob.pe/portal/)

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket slightly favors Keiko Fujimori at 31.5% over Ricardo Belmont at 26.8% for Peru's April 12 first-round presidential election, reflecting a fragmented field of 35 candidates where recent Ipsos and CPI polls show Fujimori leading 17-18% amid high undecideds exceeding 30%. Belmont's surge—from low single digits to around 6% in late surveys—stems from post-debate momentum as a populist ex-Lima mayor appealing to anti-establishment voters disillusioned by soaring crime, corruption scandals, and eight presidents since 2016. Right-wing vote splitting among Fujimori (Fuerza Popular), Rafael López Aliaga (17.5%), and Carlos Álvarez (15.5%) keeps the race tight; Lima turnout, regional splits in Arequipa and Cusco, or last-minute endorsements could propel a clear top-two pairing for the June 7 runoff.

title: # of views of next MrBeast video on week 1?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of views the next YouTube video posted by MrBeast after this market's creation gets in the first 7 days after being posted.

If MrBeast does not post a YouTube video by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest range bracket.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source for this is MrBeast's YouTube channel (https://www.youtube.com/@MrBeast), specifically the 'views' counter for the described video.

Note: This market refers to MrBeast's next video posted. Shorts, previews, or other videos released other than the referenced video will not be considered.

title: Elon Musk # tweets April 9 - April 11, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 9 12:00 PM ET to April 11, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Sevilla FC holds a slight trader consensus edge at home against a heavily depleted Atlético de Madrid in this La Liga relegation-six-pointer, with implied probabilities reflecting Atlético's injury crisis and suspensions forcing Diego Simeone into major rotation ahead of a pivotal Barcelona clash. Key Atlético absences include goalkeeper Jan Oblak (muscle), defenders Dávid Hancko and José Giménez, midfielders Johnny Cardoso and Pablo Barrios (injured), plus suspended duo Koke and Nico González, prompting nine academy call-ups and B-team reliance that weakens their typical defensive solidity. Sevilla, languishing 17th in the table, battles relegation but benefits from Ramón Sánchez-Pizjuán fortress form and lighter injury concerns beyond season-ending Marcao and doubtful César Azpilicueta; recent head-to-head favors Atlético (3-0 win in November 2025), yet current absences have traders pricing a closely contested affair with draw potential.
